title: Alice two-story house (synthetic egyptian)
description:
This two-story rectangular house, measuring 13x15 cells and 21 cells high, presents a distinct Egyptian architectural style with its prominent sandstone walls and flat roofline. The structure is largely composed of sandstone, giving it a light, sandy hue, while the interior walls and roof are constructed from spruce planks, creating a darker, contrasting brown. The roof is partially open, revealing the interior structure, and features exposed cobblestone beams that suggest a functional, rustic design. Small glass panes are visible on the exterior, providing limited fenestration. The overall impression is that of a robust, sun-baked dwelling, typical of desert climates, with a simple yet sturdy construction.

The interior of the house is designed with a ground floor and an upper floor, typical for a residential structure. The ground floor appears to be an open space, possibly for common activities, with the upper floor likely designated for private rooms. The visible interior elements include stripped spruce logs acting as support beams, and a few furnaces suggesting cooking or heating capabilities. Lanterns are hung from the ceilings, providing illumination in what otherwise appears to be a dimly lit interior due to the small windows. The presence of chests indicates storage areas. The simple layout and utilitarian furnishings convey a practical, domestic atmosphere, consistent with a functional home rather than an elaborate palace.
